# terraform-module-gcp-mssql-management

 [![Latest Release](https://img.shields.io/github/release/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-gcp-mssql-management.svg?style=for-the-badge)](https://github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-gcp-mssql-management/releases/latest) [![Last Updated](https://img.shields.io/github/last-commit/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-gcp-mssql-management.svg?style=for-the-badge)](https://github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-gcp-mssql-management/commits)


Terraform module for managing SQL Server logins and databases on GCP Cloud SQL MSSQL instances.
Login and user management is delegated to `terraform-module-mssql-management` (via an internal `module "db"` call).
This module handles GCP-side concerns: Cloud SQL connection resolution (direct or via Secret Manager),
GCP Secret Manager credential storage for each user, optional CMEK encryption, and Hoop enterprise
integration for secure per-user database access.


---

This project is part of our comprehensive approach towards DevOps Acceleration.

It's 100% Open Source and licensed under the [APACHE2](LICENSE).







We have [*lots of terraform modules*][terraform_modules] that are Open Source and we are trying to get them well-maintained!. Check them out!






## Introduction

This module provisions SQL Server databases and logins on GCP Cloud SQL MSSQL instances by delegating
the actual SQL Server management to [terraform-module-mssql-management](https://github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-mssql-management).

**Connection modes** — exactly one should be active per deployment:

| Mode | When to use |
|------|-------------|
| `cloudsql.enabled = true` with `from_secret = false` | Inline admin credentials; module constructs the Cloud SQL connection. |
| `cloudsql.enabled = true` with `from_secret = true` | Admin credentials are read from an existing GCP Secret Manager secret (JSON with `host`, `port`, `username`, `password`, `dbname` keys). |
| Direct (`direct` block, `cloudsql.enabled = false`) | Plain TCP connection to any SQL Server host. |

**GCP Secret Manager** — a `google_secret_manager_secret` resource is created for every user.
The secret contains the SQL Server login credentials generated by the base module.
Optional CMEK encryption is supported via `secrets_kms_key_name`.

**Hoop integration** — when `hoop.enabled = true` and `hoop.community = false` (enterprise mode),
the module creates additional per-user secrets (`-hoop-host`, `-hoop-port`, `-hoop-user`, `-hoop-pass`, `-hoop-db`)
and emits a `hoop_connections` output with the full connection definition consumed by the Hoop provider.
Community mode (`hoop.community = true`) creates no Hoop secrets and returns `null` for `hoop_connections`.

**Outputs**:
- `users` — map of user keys to `{ name, grant, secret_id }`.
- `databases` — map of database keys to `{ name }`.
- `hoop_connections` — Hoop connection definitions (enterprise only; `null` in community mode).

## Usage


**IMPORTANT:** The `master` branch is used in `source` just as an example. In your code, do not pin to `master` because there may be breaking changes between releases.
Instead pin to the release tag (e.g. `?ref=vX.Y.Z`) of one of our [latest releases](https://github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-gcp-mssql-management/releases).


```hcl
# terragrunt.hcl

terraform {
  source = "git::https://github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-gcp-mssql-management.git//?ref=v1.1.1"
}

inputs = {
  org = {
    organization_name = "myorg"         # (Required) Organization name.
    organization_unit = "platform"      # (Required) Organizational unit.
    environment_type  = "production"    # (Required) Environment type (e.g. production, staging).
    environment_name  = "prod"          # (Required) Short environment name.
  }

  spoke_def = "001"   # (Optional) 3-digit spoke ID. Default: "001".

  # cloudsql: (Optional) Cloud SQL MSSQL connection settings.
  # Use this block when connecting to a GCP Cloud SQL MSSQL instance.
  # Exactly one of cloudsql or direct should be active.
  cloudsql = {
    enabled        = true             # (Optional) Enable Cloud SQL mode. Default: false.
    instance_name  = "my-mssql-inst" # (Required when enabled) Cloud SQL instance name.
    project_id     = "my-gcp-proj"   # (Optional) GCP project ID. Defaults to current project.
    from_secret    = false            # (Optional) Read credentials from Secret Manager. Default: false.
    secret_id      = ""              # (Required when from_secret=true) Secret ID with JSON {host,port,username,password,dbname}.
    server_name    = "my-server"     # (Optional) Logical server name used in secret paths. Defaults to instance_name.
    admin_username = "sqladmin"      # (Optional) Admin username. Default: sqladmin.
    admin_password = "supersecret"   # (Required when enabled and from_secret=false) Admin password.
    db_name        = "master"        # (Optional) Default database. Default: master.
  }

  # direct: (Optional) Direct TCP MSSQL connection settings.
  # Used when cloudsql.enabled=false. Connects to any SQL Server by hostname.
  direct = {
    server_name = "my-server"   # (Optional) Logical server name used in secret paths.
    host        = "10.0.0.5"   # (Required) SQL Server hostname or IP address.
    port        = 1433          # (Optional) TCP port. Default: 1433.
    username    = "sa"          # (Required) Admin username.
    password    = "adminpass"   # (Required) Admin password.
    db_name     = "master"      # (Optional) Default database. Default: master.
  }

  # databases: (Optional) Map of SQL Server databases to manage.
  databases = {
    appdb = {
      name                      = "appdb"                           # (Required) Database name.
      create_owner              = true                              # (Optional) Create a dedicated owner login. Default: false.
      owner                     = "appdb_owner"                     # (Optional) Owner login name. Defaults to <name>_owner.
      default_collation         = "SQL_Latin1_General_CP1_CI_AS"   # (Optional) Database collation.
      default_language          = "us_english"                      # (Optional) Default language for the owner login.
      check_password_expiration = false                             # (Optional) Enforce password expiration. Default: false.
      check_password_policy     = true                              # (Optional) Enforce password policy. Default: true.
      must_change_password      = false                             # (Optional) Force password change on first login. Default: false.
    }
  }

  # users: (Optional) Map of SQL Server logins to create.
  users = {
    appuser = {
      name                      = "appuser"      # (Required) SQL Server login name.
      grant                     = "readwrite"    # (Required) Permission level: owner, readwrite, readonly.
      db_ref                    = "appdb"        # (Optional) Key into the databases map to resolve the database name.
      database_name             = ""             # (Optional) Database name when db_ref is not set.
      default_language          = "us_english"   # (Optional) Default: us_english.
      check_password_expiration = false          # (Optional) Default: false.
      check_password_policy     = true           # (Optional) Default: true.
      must_change_password      = false          # (Optional) Default: false.
      import                    = false          # (Optional) Import existing login instead of creating. Default: false.
      hoop = {
        access_control = []                      # (Optional) Per-user hoop access control list.
      }
    }
  }

  # hoop: (Optional) Hoop connection integration settings.
  hoop = {
    enabled        = false   # (Optional) Enable Hoop integration. Default: false.
    community      = true    # (Optional) Community (non-enterprise) mode; no per-user secrets created. Default: true.
    agent_id       = ""      # (Required in enterprise mode) Hoop agent ID.
    import         = false   # (Optional) Import existing Hoop connections. Default: false.
    tags           = {}      # (Optional) Tags to attach to Hoop connections.
    access_control = []      # (Optional) Default access control list for all connections.
  }

  password_rotation_period = 90    # (Optional) Password rotation period in days. Default: 90.
  force_reset              = false # (Optional) Force reset all passwords. Default: false.
  secrets_kms_key_name     = null  # (Optional) GCP KMS key name for CMEK encryption of Secret Manager secrets. Default: null (Google-managed keys).
}
```

## Quick Start

1. Ensure the GCP Cloud SQL MSSQL instance already exists (this module does not create it).
2. Create your `terragrunt.hcl` using the `usage` example above.
3. Choose a connection mode:
   - Set `cloudsql.enabled = true` with inline credentials, or
   - Set `cloudsql.enabled = true` and `from_secret = true` if credentials live in Secret Manager, or
   - Leave `cloudsql.enabled = false` and fill in the `direct` block for plain TCP.
4. Define your `databases` and `users` maps.
5. Run `terragrunt init && terragrunt apply`.
6. Retrieve user credentials from GCP Secret Manager using the secret IDs returned in the `users` output.
7. (Enterprise only) Use the `hoop_connections` output to register connections with the Hoop provider.


## Examples

### Cloud SQL MSSQL with Hoop enterprise integration

```hcl
# terragrunt.hcl

terraform {
  source = "git::https://github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-gcp-mssql-management.git//?ref=v1.1.1"
}

inputs = {
  org = {
    organization_name = "acme"
    organization_unit = "data"
    environment_type  = "production"
    environment_name  = "prod"
  }

  cloudsql = {
    enabled       = true
    instance_name = "prod-mssql-01"
    project_id    = "acme-prod-12345"
    from_secret   = false
    admin_username = "sqladmin"
    admin_password = "s3cr3t!"
    db_name       = "master"
  }

  databases = {
    sales = {
      name         = "salesdb"
      create_owner = true
    }
  }

  users = {
    api_user = {
      name   = "api_user"
      grant  = "readwrite"
      db_ref = "sales"
    }
  }

  hoop = {
    enabled   = true
    community = false
    agent_id  = "agt-abc123"
    tags      = { team = "data-platform" }
  }

  secrets_kms_key_name = "projects/acme-prod-12345/locations/global/keyRings/my-ring/cryptoKeys/my-key"
}
```

### Cloud SQL with credentials from Secret Manager

```hcl
inputs = {
  cloudsql = {
    enabled     = true
    instance_name = "prod-mssql-01"
    from_secret  = true
    secret_id    = "projects/acme-prod-12345/secrets/mssql-admin-creds"
  }
  # ... databases and users as above
}
```

## Inputs

| Name | Description | Type | Default | Required |
|------|-------------|------|---------|:--------:|
| <a name="input_cloudsql"></a> [cloudsql](#input\_cloudsql) | Cloud SQL MSSQL instance connection. | `any` | `{}` | no |
| <a name="input_databases"></a> [databases](#input\_databases) | Map of SQL Server databases — see inline docs. | `any` | `{}` | no |
| <a name="input_direct"></a> [direct](#input\_direct) | Direct MSSQL connection. | `any` | `{}` | no |
| <a name="input_extra_tags"></a> [extra\_tags](#input\_extra\_tags) | Extra tags to add to the resources | `map(string)` | `{}` | no |
| <a name="input_force_reset"></a> [force\_reset](#input\_force\_reset) | (Optional) Force reset passwords. Default: false. | `bool` | `false` | no |
| <a name="input_hoop"></a> [hoop](#input\_hoop) | Hoop connection settings. | `any` | `{}` | no |
| <a name="input_is_hub"></a> [is\_hub](#input\_is\_hub) | Is this a hub or spoke configuration? | `bool` | `false` | no |
| <a name="input_org"></a> [org](#input\_org) | Organization details | <pre>object({<br/>    organization_name = string<br/>    organization_unit = string<br/>    environment_type  = string<br/>    environment_name  = string<br/>  })</pre> | n/a | yes |
| <a name="input_password_rotation_period"></a> [password\_rotation\_period](#input\_password\_rotation\_period) | (Optional) Password rotation period in days. Default: 90. | `number` | `90` | no |
| <a name="input_secrets_kms_key_name"></a> [secrets\_kms\_key\_name](#input\_secrets\_kms\_key\_name) | (Optional) GCP KMS key name for encrypting secrets. | `string` | `null` | no |
| <a name="input_spoke_def"></a> [spoke\_def](#input\_spoke\_def) | Spoke ID Number, must be a 3 digit number | `string` | `"001"` | no |
| <a name="input_users"></a> [users](#input\_users) | Map of SQL Server logins — see inline docs. | `any` | `{}` | no |

## Outputs

| Name | Description |
|------|-------------|
| <a name="output_databases"></a> [databases](#output\_databases) | Databases map input reference (SQL Server databases are managed separately via mssql provider). |
| <a name="output_hoop_connections"></a> [hoop\_connections](#output\_hoop\_connections) | Hoop connection definitions (enterprise only; community returns null). |
| <a name="output_users"></a> [users](#output\_users) | Map of created SQL Server logins with their GCP Secret Manager secret IDs. |
